titleStmt.title: Letter from the Administrative Area Officer, Royal Hospital Dublin to the Chief Crown Solicitor, 14 March 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Administrative Area Officer, Royal Hospital Dublin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A series of letters between the Administrative Area Officer, Royal Hospital Dublin and the Chief Crown Solicitor, Dublin Castle concerning an injury sustained by Patrick Sheehan while he was working as a casual labourer employed by the Forage Department to handle hay, oats and straw. The series of letters concerns possible compensation as this was an injury sustained at work.

correspAction.persName: Administrative Area Officer, Royal Hospital Dublin

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Ref.No.92.
Subject.Injury to Patrick Sheehan. A.A.O., Ireland,
Royal Hos;ital ,
Dublin. 14th March, 1916 . The Chief Crown Solicitor,
Dublin Castle. Sir, In reply to your minute of 12/3/16 with
reference to Patrick Sheehan's claim for compensation,
will you please to instruct me as to what procedure I
am now to take in this matter. For you information, I attach copy of letter
received this morning from the Federation of Trade Unions
of Cork and District Health Insurance Society. Yours faithfully, Colonel,
Administrative Area Officer.
M.R.
